content.rendered:
“Let’s Talk About Us” isn’t one of Jerry Lee Lewis’ most well-known songs (released as a single in 1959, it never charted), but it’s certainly one of his most direct.

Even for Lewis, known for his suggestive lyrics, “Let’s Talk About Us” is a remarkably direct song for its time. Jerry Lee plays an impatient suitor telling his girl that he’s waited long enough: it’s sexy time.

It may not make it to a fan’s sign request let alone get selected, but Bruce did cover it once–on stage in 1982 with Dave Edmunds (who charted that year with his cover of “From Small Things (Big Things One Day Come)”) at the short-lived but legendary Big Man’s West in Red Bank.

Here’s that performance (with Bruce playing a supporting role on this one), from September 18, 1982.

Let’s Talk About Us
First performed: September 18, 1982 (Red Bank, NJ)
Last performed: September 18, 1982 (Red Bank, NJ)

© March 21, 2019

excerpt.rendered: One time only: Bruce teams up with Dave Edmunds at Big Man’s West on Jerry Lee Lewis’ “Let’s Talk About Us.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: Come On, Let’s Go
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce and Cats on a Smooth Surface cover Ritchie Valens' very first hit.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: High School Confidential
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Jerry Lee Lewis' 1958 single might have been overshadowed by personal drama and the camp classic the song introduced, but Bruce drew inspiration from it for an unusual Halloween show opener.

jetpack-related-posts.title: September 18
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Bruce debuts his cover of "Sociedade Alternativa," the first E Street Band performance of "Souls of the Departed," a tour one-off of "My Father's House," and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: Pink Cadillac
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: It may be pink, but it ain’t no car. Backstory and great performances inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Is That All to the Ball (Mr. Hall)
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Billy Lee Riley might be too obscure to cover in concert, but we can hear Bruce pay tribute in private in his 1978 and 1988 soundchecks of "Is That All to the Ball (Mr. Hall."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Guitar Man
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joins Sting for a rocking cover of Jerry Reed's and Elvis Presley's 1967 single.
